# convex-doctor

> Deterministic static analysis for Convex codebases. It catches the runtime errors TypeScript can't see — validator drift, table-scanning queries, dead functions — and every finding ships with a fix recipe an agent can apply. Built to be driven by AI agents: "Convex is the best backend for your agent. convex-doctor keeps it that way."

convex-doctor runs one pass over any Convex project before deploy — no install, no config, CI-ready. It is distributed on npm as `@fagnersales/convex-doctor` and run with `bunx @fagnersales/convex-doctor`. MIT licensed. Not affiliated with Convex, Inc.

## What it checks

- **Drift engine**: diffs schema → validator → every return path (through joins, spreads, `.paginate()` envelopes, and cross-file imports) to catch validator drift that TypeScript can't see but Convex throws on at runtime. Codes: `Missing_field`, `Stale_field`, `Null_branch_missing`, `Optionality_mismatch`, `Cardinality_mismatch`, `Type_mismatch`, `Extra_literal_field`, `Missing_literal_field`.
- **Best-practice lints**: the Convex best-practices guide, the official eslint-plugin rules, and rules they don't ship — one pass, no config, each diagnostic with why/fix/doc link. Rules include `Filter_in_query`, `Await_in_loop`, `Unbounded_collect`, `Fetch_in_query`, `Db_in_action`, `Nondeterministic_query`, `Schedule_public_fn`, `Floating_ctx_promise`, `Missing_arg_validator`, `Query_in_node_file`, `Duplicate_cron_id`, `Redundant_index`, `Wrong_runtime_import`, `Node_builtin_without_use_node`, `Misplaced_use_node`, `Sequential_ctx_run`, `Cron_public_fn`, `Old_function_syntax`, `Schema_validation_disabled`, and more.
- **Dead-function detection** (`--dead`): builds a project-wide call graph and lists every function no caller reaches. Resolves `api.*`/`internal.*` chains through barrel re-exports, counts `"path/file:fn"` string literals as callers, ignores self-calls, and honors `// convex-doctor: keep` comments to preserve manual entry points.

## Installation

There is no install step and no config file. convex-doctor runs on [Bun](https://bun.sh) — the only prerequisite — and is executed directly with `bunx`:

- Audit any Convex project: `bunx @fagnersales/convex-doctor`
- Non-default Convex dir: `bunx @fagnersales/convex-doctor --convex-dir backend/convex`
- Full/strict CI run: `bunx @fagnersales/convex-doctor --dead --strict --json`
- Exit codes: `0` no errors (deploy away); `1` errors found (or warnings under `--strict`); `2` bad arguments.

Wire it into typecheck so it runs on every push (`package.json`):

```jsonc
"scripts": {
  "doctor": "bunx @fagnersales/convex-doctor",
  "typecheck": "tsgo --noEmit && bun doctor"
}
```

## CLI reference

```
groups                   List fixable groups (one per rule code), priority-ordered
agent-guide              Print the agentic fix-loop recipe
--convex-dir <path>      Path to convex/ directory. Default: convex
--schema <path>          Path to schema.ts. Default: <convex-dir>/schema.ts
--only <code|category>   Restrict the report to one rule code or category (the agentic unit)
--limit <n>              Cap --only work-lists (default 20; 0 = all)
--include-unanalyzed     Print INFO entries for unanalyzed handlers
--json                   Emit JSON instead of text (versioned, CI-friendly)
--strict                 Exit nonzero if any warnings are present
--no-lint                Skip the best-practice lints (drift checks only)
--dead                   Print the dead-function list after the regular report
--dead-only              Print only the dead list
--ignore-dead <pattern>  Glob excluding entry points from the dead list. Repeatable
--project-root <path>    Root scanned for callers. Default: parent of <convex-dir>
-h, --help               Show help
```

## Suppressing findings

- Silence one finding, with a reason: `// convex-doctor: ignore <CODE> — reason` above the flagged line. The code is required; a different issue on the same line still surfaces.
- Keep a dead-listed entry point alive: `// convex-doctor: keep — reason` above the export. Kept and `--ignore-dead <glob>` functions are live roots — everything they call stays alive too.

## Programmatic API

```ts
import { run, reportText, exitCode } from "@fagnersales/convex-doctor";

const result = run({ convexDir: "convex", lint: true });
console.log(reportText(result));
process.exit(exitCode(result, false));
```

`reportJson` and `buildGraph` are exported alongside `run`.

## Agentic fix loop

convex-doctor is designed for an agent to fix its own findings one rule code at a time instead of dumping every issue into a context window:

1. List fixable groups: `bunx @fagnersales/convex-doctor groups --json`
2. Lock one group: `bunx @fagnersales/convex-doctor --only <CODE> --json` (returns the shared recipe plus file · line · function per site)
3. Fix every site with the group's recipe, then re-scan until the group reads zero, then commit. Repeat until the scan is clean.

Each group is tagged by how much judgment the fix needs: **Mechanical** (diagnostic fully determines the edit, safe to apply blind), **Guided** (deterministic recipe, read local context first), **Manual** (cross-file or data-migration judgment, may need a human).

- Convex best practices: https://docs.convex.dev/understanding/best-practices/
